session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8626 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback using the command specified in the skill:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>&1 || git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>&1
Get file-level restructuring context for the current branch

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8626, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8626", "pr_title": "Fix flaky timestamp test in `test_output_modes.py` by injecting time dependency", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xn6bw", "path": "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py", "line": 722, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Dignified Code Simplifier Review**: Default parameter value `now: datetime | None = None` - dignified-python prohibits default parameter values. Use keyword-only args without defaults and check at callsites. See dignified-python/references/api-design.md", "created_at": "2026-03-02T18:50:48Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xn61R", "path": "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py", "line": 724, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Dignified Python**: Default parameter values are dangerous - Remove default and require explicit injection. All callers pass `now=` explicitly, so the default is unnecessary. See dignified-python/references/api-design.md.", "created_at": "2026-03-02T18:51:19Z" }

session.tool_result:
720→ 721→def ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date( 722→ base_name: str, worktrees_dir: Path, git_ops, *, now: datetime | None = None 723→) -> str: 724→ """Ensure unique worktree name with datetime suffix and smart versioning. 725→ 726→ Adds datetime suffix in format -YY-MM-DD-HHMM to the base name. 727→ If a worktree with that name exists, increments numeric suffix starting at 2 AFTER the datetime. 728→ Uses LBYL pattern: checks via git_ops.worktree.path_exists() before operations. 729→ 730→ This function is used for plan-derived worktrees where multiple worktrees may be 731→ created from the same plan, requiring datetime-based disambiguation. 732→ 733→ Args: 734→ base_name: Sanitized worktree base name (without datetime suffix)
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

790→ 791→def ensure_unique_worktree_name( 792→ base_name: str, worktrees_dir: Path, git_ops, *, now: datetime | None = None 793→) -> str: 794→ """Deprecated: Use ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date for plan-derived worktrees. 795→ 796→ This function is kept for backward compatibility but will be removed in the future. 797→ New code should use: 798→ - ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date() for plan-derived worktrees 799→ - ensure_simple_worktree_name() for manual checkout operations 800→ """ 801→ return ensure_unique_worktree_name_with_date(base_name, worktrees_dir, git_ops, now=now) 802→ 803→ 804→def default_branch_for_worktree(name: str) -> str:
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
